CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-20-2009 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Consolidated with Case NO. 471140 Horstmeyer vs. Ng. Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed. The court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings reported by: Karen Farnsworth, CSR# 5162. (212)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-21-2008 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-20-09 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-02-2008 Continued To Dec-08-08 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

Hearing On Petition To Approve The Compromise Of A Disputed Claim Of A Minor.
Off Calendar Sep-09-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor.
Dept. 218, Comm. Frank Drago, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

William C. Spain, Esq. appeared with petitioner Vith Ham, who was present as Guardian ad litem for the minor Lisa Ham, who was not present at today's hearing.
Petitioner was placed under oath and examined.
Tommy Ham was placed under oath and served as Cambodian speaking interpreter for petitioner.
David G. Hysinger, Esq. appeared for defendant Collin Alexander Ng, who was not present at today's hearing.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved.

Settlement Amount - $300,000.00.
Attorneys' Fees - $22,340.00.
Medical Expenses - $2,660.00.

A net balance of $225,000.00 shall be distributed as follows: $15,000.00 shall be deposited into a blocked account with Bank of America, Oakland Chinatown Branch, located at 388 9th Street, Oakland, CA. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order, or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years. The remaining $210,000.00 shall be used to purchase an annuity with American Life Insurance Company as described in Attachments 16-1 and 16-2 to Petitioner's Third Amendment to the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor.

Orders Approving Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor, and to Deposit Money Into Blocked, were submitted to and signed by the Court.
